mnemonics.title: Unadjusted Function Point (UFP) Formula
mnemonics.content: UFP = (ILF x 7) + (EIF x 5) + (EI x 3) + (EO x 4) + (EQ x 3)

acronyms.usage: Used to measure the size and complexity of a software system
Used to calculate the Function Point value
Used to adjust the Function Point value based on the complexity of the software system
Used to calculate the Unadjusted Function Point value

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a software system as a house, and the Function Point Metrics as a way to measure the size and complexity of the house
Imagine a software system as a puzzle, and the Unadjusted Function Point as a way to calculate the number of puzzle pieces
Imagine a software system as a car, and the Value Adjustment Factor as a way to adjust the speed of the car based on the road conditions

analogies.analogy: Measuring the size and complexity of a software system is like measuring the size and complexity of a house
Calculating the Unadjusted Function Point value is like counting the number of puzzle pieces
Adjusting the Function Point value based on the complexity of the software system is like adjusting the speed of a car based on the road conditions
